---
title: "Reference"
---

# Public functions

The package keeps the public surface small on purpose.

## Main entry points

| Function | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `path_features(path, depth = 4)` | Return the paper-facing path coordinates |
| `classical_features(path)` | Return classical line-profile summaries |
| `as_spectral_path(u, F)` | Build a path matrix from ordered coordinate and signal |
| `emabs_area(path)` | Compute the emission–absorption ordering coordinate |

## Paper notation

| Output column | Interpretation |
|---|---|
| `p2` | signed velocity–flux area |
| `p3u` | location of asymmetry along the ordered coordinate |
| `p3F` | whether asymmetry is concentrated in bright or faint parts of the line |
| `p4F` | higher-order flux modulation |
| `p4T` | twist-like higher-order structure |
| `p_pm` | emission–absorption ordering |

For descriptive names instead of paper notation:

```r
path_features(path, notation = "descriptive")
```

## Minimal workflow

```r
library(spectropath)

u <- seq(-5, 5, length.out = 500)
f <- dnorm(u, 0, 1) + 0.25 * dnorm(u, 1.8, 0.45)
path <- cbind(u, f)

path_features(path)
classical_features(path)
```

## Scope

`spectropath` is the narrow, paper-facing package. It is meant for ordered
line-profile diagnostics with stable notation, rather than the broader
exploratory feature space that existed in earlier experiments.
